extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won the Matrah Corniche finish after HAAS Nathan and Katusha tried to explode the race on the final climb but failed to create selection
tactical_patterns: Katusha-Alpecin's HAAS attempted to split the sprint field on the final climb approach, revealing tactical desperation when lacking pure speed
CAVAGNA Remy (Quick-Step) went solo from the five-man break on the Matrah Corniche circuit, taking maximum risks on technical descent laps
Trek-Segafredo worked to control the break for NIZZOLO despite not having dominant sprint form, suggesting limited team options

rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: Dominated this specific Matrah Corniche finish all week, indicating course-specific advantage on technical uphill sprint finishes
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: negative: Katusha's attempt to pre-emptively attack before the sprint finish suggests they lacked confidence in pure leadout speed
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: positive: Strong enough to solo away from established breakaway on repeated Matrah climbs, showing unusually aggressive racing for Quick-Step domestique
rider_signals.LUTSENKO Alexei: positive: Defended 11-second GC advantage through technical circuit finish, showing time-trial strength translated to position defense
